Author: R Thompson
Title: Three Parallel AI Agents, One Merge — And We Built a Production-Ready Feature in an Afternoon
Text:
Member-only story
Three Parallel AI Agents, One Merge — And We Built a Production-Ready Feature in an Afternoon
The new playbook for lightning-fast product sprints.
“The computer is going to end up writing more code than any human ever will — and the question is: will you be directing it, or debugging its mistakes?”
At 2:13 a.m., my screen stopped blinking.
No errors. No warnings. Just a clean, obedient green line where red had ruled for hours.
I hadn’t typed the final fix.
Claude did.
Not the chatty, browser-tab Claude that suggests polite snippets and waits for approval. This was Claude Code Desktop — the version that doesn’t ask so much as it acts. That night was the moment I realized something subtle and irreversible had happened to software development. We didn’t just add a smarter assistant to the workflow. We handed part of the workflow over.
What follows isn’t a product tour. It’s a field report from the front edge of agentic software — the strange new zone where code no longer waits for hands to move.
